name: Pina Colada Cake
recipeInstructions:
Note: The sweetened coconut cream is the canned type.
For Cake:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Butter two 9-inch round cake pans and line with parchment paper.
3. Sift together flour, 1 cup sugar, baking powder, and salt.
4. Whisk yolks, oil, water, and vanilla in a large bowl until soft peaks form.
5. Gradually add the remaining 1/2 cup sugar and continue beating until stiff but not dry.
6. Fold the dry ingredients into the yolk mixture.
7. Gently fold in beaten whites.
8. Pour batter into prepared pans.
9. Bake until lightly golden and tops spring back when touched, about 30 minutes.
10. Invert cakes onto racks and cool.
For Frosting:
1. Mix pineapple juice, coconut cream, and rum in a small bowl.
2. Brush the tops of the cakes with the juice mixture.
3. Using an electric mixer, beat powdered sugar, butter, cream, egg, vanilla, and coconut extract in a large bowl until smooth and creamy.
4. Transfer 1 cup frosting to a small bowl. Stir in crushed pineapple.
5. Add rum to the remaining frosting in the large bowl.
6. Set one cake layer on a plate. Spread with pineapple frosting.
7. Top with the second cake layer, smooth side up.
8. Frost the top and sides of the cake with as much of the remaining frosting as desired.
9. Sprinkle the top of the cake with coconut.
